So if we take all of, I see you've arrived, Michael. So if we take all of Michael Jans' PNC guys, who he has provided with marketing systems and sales tools and all that, so he's taken a microcosm out of the PNC insurance industry and organized him over here. Guess what? Same pyramid now can be found in his customer base. Same damn pyramid. There's 1% of them kicking ass, taking names, using everything he's given them. Their income's multiplied. They just bought their second yacht. They got their second mistress. They got their third wife. They're just flying. There's 4% who are better. And then we start down the pyramid. If we go take the Forbes 500, which they publish every year of the richest Americans, go take a look at it. Their net worth is in it. It's 500 richest Americans. Take the net worth and build yourself a little pyramid. It's a pyramid. There's 1% of the 500 who are enormously rich. There's another 4% who are impressively rich. And then you're all the way down to the bottom of that 500, the guys who pretty much need to keep running their business still working just to cover their overhead. Now they got a big nut, but the same pyramid is there. So we are not ever gonna impact, now we'll impact individuals, because obviously we all probably, at least most of us probably, started in one place of the pyramid and now we're in a different place. But by the way, most of us would've got there regardless of whether whoever influenced us existed or not, because we would've found somebody else. The influences are really interchangeable. It's about the person being influenced, not really the influencer. So if I had enough money to go to college and become a veterinarian, instead of doing this, the impact on any of you or the world would be non-existent. It wouldn't matter, because somebody else would've influenced you and you would've done what you did anyway. It's not, and the same thing's true of you, by the way. You are an interchangeable doohickey. If you went and did something else, ultimately, all of Michael's P and C ages, 99% of them who are now in the 5% would've found their way to the 5%, whether through him or whoever was his replacement. But we are not, so we move individuals up and down, but the numbers never change, never. And so, both process, profitability, business structure, it all, message, it all has to reflect the reality of this pyramid. If it is ever built under the delusion that it is somehow going to take the bottom of this pyramid and invert it, and that's how you're gonna buy your mansion, that's delusional, not gonna happen. And by the way, the big, big, big, big movements who are all about that, namely Christianity, which is all about inverting the pyramid, that's the whole point, right? Losing ground, not gaining ground, especially here in the United States. So church attendance is down, donation, it's all down. It's all trending down, it's not trending up. So inverting the pyramid, insane. And you gotta be careful that nothing you're doing in your business is about the delusion of inverting the pyramid, not gonna happen. Our business has to be about finding the ones who are or can be developed into the top part of that pyramid. I'm gonna skip over the Halbert stuff, but it all has to do with the power of the list. You can get to it on your own. So I'm all the way over to the stuff that looks like this about the Faith Healer's List, but I'll tell you one other thing before we get there. There's a book, if you read the book, Sham, how many of you read the book, Sham? Oh, very good, good for you. Okay, so in it he was talking about his experience at Rodale and very important, so I'm just gonna read it real quick. So guided our entire approach at Rodale, the most likely customer for a book on any given topic with someone who had bought a similar book within the preceding 18 months. I have more, but so let's just stop there for a second. In list language, which in about another 45 minutes, where Craig's gonna talk to you about direct mail list, in list language, that's recency. It's a select if you go get direct mail list. So you could get the last 90 day buyer, you can get the last 12 months buyer, you can get the last 18 months buyer. With subscription list, you can get the recent expires, people who expired and did not renew in the last 90 days, which often for many of us is the best list because they've given up on Guru A, but it doesn't mean they've given up on topic A, they just have given up on Guru A, which is why companies have portfolios of newsletters so they can move the expires to another one that they own. That's what that business is all about, much like the faith healer was. So a person who had bought a similar book within the preceding 18 months. Now, first of all, it's important to know whatever comparable truth exists about your business and about your customer. So I just gave you one of the big truths that exist about all your businesses, which is the shape of the pyramid and the fact that it won't change. This may be a truth about your business, it may not, but there is a truth like it about your business and who the most likely customer is. And it's important to know the truth and then build everything for the most likely customer. So this gets us all the way to like length of message, right? So here's what everybody tells me, nobody, just like George said last night, so he and I talk about this a lot because we refuse to be dissuaded. So.
